description_long: This government building features a grand, symmetrical U-shaped layout with a prominent central dome and classical architectural detailing. The facade is adorned with a series of evenly spaced windows and pilasters, giving it a stately and formal appearance. The central section projects outward and is topped by a large dome, flanked by two wings that extend back and form an open courtyard. The roofline is flat with subtle parapets, and the main entrance is accentuated by a portico with columns and decorative elements. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from stone or concrete, with large window arrays suggesting ample natural light inside. The overall design conveys authority and permanence, typical of major civic or governmental institutions.
description: A grand, U-shaped government building with a central dome, classical columns, and symmetrical wings forming an open courtyard. The facade is formal, with many windows and ornate architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central dome, main entrance with columns, and symmetrical wings extending back.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the wings and the dome's profile, with long rows of windows visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the open courtyard formed by the U-shape and the back of the central dome.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the dome and extended wing visible.
attribute_public_entrance_design: The main public entrance is marked by a grand portico with classical columns and an ornate pediment, providing a formal and imposing entryway. Wide steps lead up to the entrance, emphasizing accessibility and prominence.
